title: Royal Australian Air Force plane in rare visit to Prestwick

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: The Royal Australian Air Force Airbus A330-200MRTT turned heads as it made a brief refuelling stop (Image: Martin Clarke) The Royal Australian Air Force Airbus A330-200MRTT turned heads as it made a brief refuelling stop at the airport on Thursday, March 5. The KC-30A Multi-Role Tanker Transport (MRTT) is a heavily modified Airbus A330 airliner that allows the Royal Australian Air Force to conduct air-to-air refuelling and provide strategic air lift.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: More : Special military aircraft from Middle East turns heads at Prestwick Airport It features advanced communication and navigation systems, and an electronic warfare self-protection system for shielding against threats from surface-to-air missiles. The Air Force's seven KC-30A aircraft are based at RAAF Base Amberley, operated by No. 33 Squadron.
